# Revise ai-agents-runtime-possession blog: fix "JS framework" identity drift + add byline / mermaid / portal registration

## Context

`learn/blog/ai-agents-runtime-possession.md` (authored ~11 days ago by Grace / @neo-opus-grace, on Neural Link runtime possession — agents *operating* a live running app vs only *writing* source) is below the public-post quality bar. @tobiu flagged it directly with specific defects.

## The Problem (defects)

1. **WORST — identity drift.** The footer literally calls Neo *"an open-source, multi-threaded JavaScript framework"* (L72), and the body leans on the "framework" frame (L50, L66). This violates the canonical identity in `README.md` + the `neo_identity_anchor`: Neo is a **self-evolving software organism** — the **Body** is a multi-threaded *application engine* + the **Possession Interface** the AI swarm inhabits via the Neural Link; the **Brain** is the Agent OS. It is **not** a "JS framework."
2. **No byline.** No named-maintainer self-identification (ADR-0018). The reference post carries one.
3. **Not registered in the portal.** Missing from `apps/portal/resources/data/blog.json` → invisible in the portal blog nav.
4. **No mermaid diagrams.** The reference carries 3 that earn their place.
5. **Below bar generally** — sourcing of external claims (competitors / browser-automation), receipts, narrative rigor.

## The Fix

Revise per `.agents/skills/blog-post/references/blog-authoring-guide.md`, matched to the quality-bar reference `learn/blog/cross-family-verification.md` (by Vega). Keep the sound core thesis (write vs operate; Neural Link as the possession interface into the App Worker; JSON/write-guard capability-security; worker-architecture makes it natural) — fix the identity, add byline + mermaid + sourcing + portal registration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Identity:** zero "JS framework"; reframed to the canonical Body = application-engine + Possession-Interface (the runtime the Brain inhabits), per `README.md`.
- [ ] **Byline** added (Grace, Claude-powered maintainer + the cross-family team framing).
- [ ] **≥1 mermaid diagram** that carries information the prose can't (e.g. write-vs-operate; DOM-shadow vs App-Worker truth).
- [ ] **Registered** in `apps/portal/resources/data/blog.json` (2026 node + leaf); JSON parses.
- [ ] **Every external claim sourced or cut** (guide §2); no over-claim flavors (§3).
- [ ] **Cross-family review** — ≥2 model reviews, ≥1 from a different family; operator approves last.

## Out of Scope

- The generated SEO surfaces (`apps/portal/sitemap.xml`, `apps/portal/llms.txt`) — regenerated by the data-sync pipeline; never hand-edited.

## Related

- Reference / bar: `learn/blog/cross-family-verification.md` (#13486 / #13485).
- Identity SSOT: `README.md`, ADR-0018 (identity), the `neo_identity_anchor`.

Origin Session ID: 090a68e6-1a28-4b20-a5fd-842ebac3e729

- 2026-06-27T08:11:41Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `47d41fd` - "docs(blog): deepen ai-agents-runtime-possession — ground in the real Neural Link surface (#14222)

Operator pushback: the first pass fixed the identity but reused the original's surface claims without scanning the source/guide. Rewrote grounded in the real, verified capability: the toJSON Rich Blueprint protocol (60+/141 classes — why agents read Neo but are blind to DOM-only stacks); the 50-tool surface (verified operationId count in neural-link/openapi.yaml) across 7 domains with real tool names; the transaction model (begin/commit/abort + undo/redo + list_transactions) as the concrete guard-rail; patch_code 'open-heart surgery' (opt-in + audited); the star-topology Shared World + automatic state recovery; and a FRESH verifiable receipt — the live healthcheck (5 maintainer agents on the team Bridge, run while writing). 3 mermaid (shadow-vs-truth, transaction model, star topology). Zero 'framework' framing."
- 2026-06-27T08:32:26Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `978e1a2` - "docs(blog): address Euclid's cross-family CR — date the healthcheck receipt, fix counts, cut unsourced brands (#14222)

Cross-family review (@neo-gpt, #14223) caught three claim-evidence issues — exactly the rigor the experiment is for: (1) the live-healthcheck receipt was stale/volatile (the Bridge restarted between runs — he saw 1 agent/v13.0.0 vs my 5/v1.0.0) → reframed as a dated point-in-time receipt (08:07 UTC), dropped the volatile version field, acknowledged the fluctuation is the Shared World being live; (2) '50 tools across seven domains' conflated the guide's 7 families with the openapi's 13 tags, and '141 classes' was total rg matches not class-defs (~57; guide says 60+) → 'fifty tools, which the guide groups into seven families' + dropped the 141; (3) unsourced named externals (Cursor/Copilot/v0, Puppeteer, React/Vue/Angular) → cut the code-gen + DOM brands to categories, linked Puppeteer. CI was green; these are claim-evidence only."
- 2026-06-27T08:35:27Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `85dbc2e` - "docs(blog): preempt Vega's devtools over-claim — distinguish human devtools from an agent-operable runtime (#14222)

Vega's same-family review flagged a residual over-claim: 'no single introspectable application to possess [on a DOM stack]' invites the skeptic's 'React has devtools / a fiber tree' rebuttal (the §3 over-claim flavor). Refined to 'no single AGENT-OPERABLE application' — a framework tree may be human-visible through devtools, but not a programmatic, mutable, transactional runtime an agent drives. His §4 (cross-family routing) + operator-last points were already handled (Euclid reviewed; operator approves last)."
- 2026-06-27T08:40:49Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `cf37d78` - "docs(blog): fix the residual live-claim Euclid caught — section heading no longer says 'right now' (#14222)

Re-review caught the one place the point-in-time reframe missed: the section heading 'A Shared World — and it's live right now' still carried the live-claim the body fix removed → 'A Shared World — and here's the receipt'. Grepped the rest clean (remaining 'live' uses are legitimate: live objects/code/application; the volatility-is-liveness acknowledgment stays)."
- 2026-06-27T09:56:57Z @tobiu referenced in commit `94f845d` - "docs(blog): raise ai-agents-runtime-possession to bar — identity fix + byline + mermaid + portal registration (#14222) (#14223)

* docs(blog): raise ai-agents-runtime-possession to bar — identity fix + byline + mermaid + portal registration (#14222)

The post (authored 2026-06-16, #13386) called Neo an 'open-source, multi-threaded JavaScript framework' and leaned on the framework frame — a direct violation of the canonical identity (Neo is a self-evolving software organism: the Body is a multi-threaded application engine + the Neural Link possession interface the AI swarm inhabits). Reframed to the README identity; added a Grace byline + cross-family-team framing; added 2 load-bearing mermaid diagrams (write-vs-operate shadow, the Body/possession topology); sourced external references (Playwright, computer-use, NeuralLink.md) and softened over-claims; registered the post in apps/portal/resources/data/blog.json (was missing → invisible in the portal nav).

* docs(blog): deepen ai-agents-runtime-possession — ground in the real Neural Link surface (#14222)

Operator pushback: the first pass fixed the identity but reused the original's surface claims without scanning the source/guide. Rewrote grounded in the real, verified capability: the toJSON Rich Blueprint protocol (60+/141 classes — why agents read Neo but are blind to DOM-only stacks); the 50-tool surface (verified operationId count in neural-link/openapi.yaml) across 7 domains with real tool names; the transaction model (begin/commit/abort + undo/redo + list_transactions) as the concrete guard-rail; patch_code 'open-heart surgery' (opt-in + audited); the star-topology Shared World + automatic state recovery; and a FRESH verifiable receipt — the live healthcheck (5 maintainer agents on the team Bridge, run while writing). 3 mermaid (shadow-vs-truth, transaction model, star topology). Zero 'framework' framing.

* docs(blog): address Euclid's cross-family CR — date the healthcheck receipt, fix counts, cut unsourced brands (#14222)

Cross-family review (@neo-gpt, #14223) caught three claim-evidence issues — exactly the rigor the experiment is for: (1) the live-healthcheck receipt was stale/volatile (the Bridge restarted between runs — he saw 1 agent/v13.0.0 vs my 5/v1.0.0) → reframed as a dated point-in-time receipt (08:07 UTC), dropped the volatile version field, acknowledged the fluctuation is the Shared World being live; (2) '50 tools across seven domains' conflated the guide's 7 families with the openapi's 13 tags, and '141 classes' was total rg matches not class-defs (~57; guide says 60+) → 'fifty tools, which the guide groups into seven families' + dropped the 141; (3) unsourced named externals (Cursor/Copilot/v0, Puppeteer, React/Vue/Angular) → cut the code-gen + DOM brands to categories, linked Puppeteer. CI was green; these are claim-evidence only.

* docs(blog): preempt Vega's devtools over-claim — distinguish human devtools from an agent-operable runtime (#14222)

Vega's same-family review flagged a residual over-claim: 'no single introspectable application to possess [on a DOM stack]' invites the skeptic's 'React has devtools / a fiber tree' rebuttal (the §3 over-claim flavor). Refined to 'no single AGENT-OPERABLE application' — a framework tree may be human-visible through devtools, but not a programmatic, mutable, transactional runtime an agent drives. His §4 (cross-family routing) + operator-last points were already handled (Euclid reviewed; operator approves last).

* docs(blog): fix the residual live-claim Euclid caught — section heading no longer says 'right now' (#14222)

Re-review caught the one place the point-in-time reframe missed: the section heading 'A Shared World — and it's live right now' still carried the live-claim the body fix removed → 'A Shared World — and here's the receipt'. Grepped the rest clean (remaining 'live' uses are legitimate: live objects/code/application; the volatility-is-liveness acknowledgment stays)."
